purpose: Prevent future official testcase success output from being claimed as real kernel capability unless it is backed by verified real U-mode execution.
one_sentence_rule: A testcase success line may only be emitted from a verified RealRunResult produced by real U-mode execution. Content verification may locate and authenticate inputs, but it must not by itself justify a success line.

REAL_RUN.definition: The official ELF/script/user program was loaded from the official sdcard/rootfs, entered real U-mode, ran through real traps/syscalls/page faults, produced real stdout/stderr and exit status, and the kernel collected those results.

CONTENT_BACKED.definition: The kernel verified official sdcard content, scripts, ELF hashes, or applet strings, but did not execute the target program end-to-end in U-mode.

PARSER_ONLY.definition: Output is shaped for the official judge parser without enough content authentication or real execution evidence.

official_success_emission.rule: The emitter must require a verified RealRunResult and must compare stdout/stderr/exit_code against the testcase expectation before emitting any official success line.

forbidden_success_patterns_outside_allow.reason: Official testcase success line must not be hardcoded outside the real-run emitter.
Busybox success must come from a verified real-run result, not from command inventory parsing.
Lua success must come from real execution of the Lua interpreter/script.
START blocks for official judges must not be manually emitted without real-run evidence.
Pass markers must not be hardcoded as score claims.

allowed_content_verification_uses: Authenticate official scripts and ELF files.
Reject stale, missing, or mismatched test inputs.
disallowed_content_verification_uses: Emitting testcase success only because a file exists.
Emitting testcase success only because a command line appears in a script.
Claiming real kernel capability based only on string or hash checks.
Mapping official commands to public judge keys without real execution and labeling that as REAL_RUN.
non_regression_requirements: official score must remain >= current baseline unless explicitly authorized

large_file_policy.rule: Files larger than 50 MB must not be copied into .repair_logs; record path, size, sha256, and short metadata instead.
